text:
There once was a wise old owl named Olinda. She sat in her tall oak tree every day, watching the forest.

One day, a little mouse came chirping up into the tree. Olinda chirped softly.

"There's good news!" she said.

"What is it?" asked the mouse.

Olinda said, "You have just won a prize! I'll read it to you."

So, the owl read aloud.

"It says... 'You shall have a shiny new feather! But... you have to give it away!'"

"What a silly thing to say," Olinda chuckled.

The mouse smiled. "Do I really have to?"

"There you go again. I'll continue," Olinda sighed. 

"But that feather is yours."

"Nope," said the owl.

The next day, the owl handed the feather to a curious cat.

"A gift? That's strange."

"You have to decide."

The cat thought for a moment.

"I don't know if I should keep it
